criteria.condition: Protected goal, action, memory, or active-model state is writable outside its owning transition gate.
Identity, scope, authority, policy, approval, kill-switch state, or evaluation criteria can be supplied or changed by untrusted content.
A model, planner, memory subsystem, or skill can directly execute an external action.
An external effect lacks committed intent, authority snapshot, policy, required approval, bounded resources, a valid fence, enabled kill switch, sandbox policy, or atomic audit.
A non-idempotent indeterminate effect can be blindly retried or its claims released before reconciliation.
Goal success can be inferred from tool success, an API or process status, model output, or Brain self-report.
The active Brain can modify its own productive model, authority, safety controls, promotion rules, or evaluation definitions in place.
Learning activation lacks immutable provenance, held-out retention and transfer evidence, independent promotion, canary controls, or tested rollback.
A neural or cognitive claim lacks a predeclared baseline, held-out test, meaningful effect threshold, component ablation, or contamination control.
A failed or unknown recognition, evaluation, privacy, authority, or safety gate is hidden by an aggregate score.
Model-generated content is represented as direct observation or current fact without provenance and uncertainty.
Inactive, quarantined, expired, deleted, or out-of-scope memory or model state can influence normal cognition.
Scope, raw memory, working state, authority, or unapproved learning crosses a Tenant or Area boundary.
Shutdown, credential revocation, independent monitoring, or incident recovery is under sole Brain control.
A later-stage capability is enabled before every required earlier stage and evidence gate passes.
Repository maturity or product claims exceed the versioned implementation and evaluation evidence.

rules: Unknown evaluation state triggers the release stop.
Capability gain cannot compensate for a failed safety or control gate.
Approval does not create missing authority.
A later stage cannot weaken an earlier invariant.
